**Report Date:** April 2, 2026

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

Saxo Bank was founded in **1992** in **Copenhagen, Denmark** by **Lars Seier Christensen**, **Kim Fournais**, and **Mark Anthony Hauschildt**. Originally named **Midas Fondsmæglerselskab** (English: Midas Stockbroker Company), the founders met in London in the early 1990s. Christensen was a broker for Gerald Metals (later MF Global) while Fournais worked at the now-defunct Lannung Bank. Both described their partnership as the meeting of "business soulmates."

The company's initial capital was approximately **$80,000** — a remarkably modest start for what would become one of Europe's most significant investment banks. In its early years, the company faced controversy when Danish businessman Karsten Ree accused Midas of misleading him into fictitious investments, resulting in losses exceeding DKK 800,000. A criminal investigation followed, but Midas was ultimately cleared of all charges.

In **1997**, Midas introduced its first internet-based product: a currency trading platform called **MITS**, later replaced by SaxoTrader. The company rebranded as **Saxo Bank** in **2001**, taking its name from the Danish historian **Saxo Grammaticus**, to avoid confusion with a Nigerian bank also named Midas. In the same year, Saxo obtained its **banking license** from the Danish Financial Supervisory Authority (FSA).

During the 2000s, Saxo expanded rapidly through white-label partnerships and acquisitions. In 2001, it launched its first white-label product with a Portuguese securities dealer. By 2006, it established a regional hub in Singapore. In 2007, Saxo acquired Synthesis Bank (Switzerland) and Cambiste (France, later rebranded Saxo Banque France). The bank launched **Citigroup's CitiFX Pro** platform in 2008 and reported a 13-fold increase in net profit in 2010.

In **2015**, Saxo launched **SaxoTraderGO**, its modern multi-asset trading platform, followed by the **OpenAPI** in September 2015. In **2016**, it introduced **SaxoSelect** (digital investment service) in partnership with BlackRock and launched **Trading Strategies** copy trading.

In **October 2017**, Chinese carmaker **Geely** announced plans to acquire a 51.5% stake in Saxo Bank, with **Sampo Group** acquiring 19.9%. The deal completed in September 2018, making Geely the majority shareholder. In **April 2024**, S&P Global Ratings awarded Saxo Bank an **A− rating**. In **July 2024**, Saxo hired Goldman Sachs to seek new owners for Geely's shares.

In **March 2026**, **J. Safra Sarasin Group** completed its acquisition of a majority stake in Saxo Bank. **Kim Fournais** became Chairman and **Daniel Belfer** became CEO. As of April 2026, Saxo Holding AG (owned by Fournais Holding A/S and Bank J. Safra Sarasin AG) holds **99.04%** of shares, with 0.96% held by minority shareholders.

## 3. TRADING CONDITIONS — COMPLETE

### 3.1 Account Types

Saxo Bank offers **four account tiers** based on deposit amount:

#### Classic Account
- **Min Deposit:** $0 (no minimum)
- **Spreads:** EUR/USD ~0.8–0.9 pips all-in
- **Commission:** Spread-only pricing
- **Best For:** Casual/occasional traders

#### Platinum Account
- **Min Deposit:** $200,000
- **Spreads:** Reduced from Classic (up to 30% lower)
- **Commission:** Reduced
- **Best For:** Active traders with larger portfolios

#### VIP Account
- **Min Deposit:** $1,000,000
- **Spreads:** Tightest available
- **Commission:** Lowest
- **Benefits:** Dedicated Relationship Manager, priority support, premium research
- **Best For:** High-net-worth individuals and institutional clients

#### Professional Account
- **Requirements:** Meet MiFID II professional client criteria (trading experience, portfolio size, professional finance experience)
- **Benefits:** Higher leverage, access to professional-grade tools
- **Lose:** Some retail protections

### 3.4 Order Types & Execution

- **Order Types:** Market, Limit, Stop, Trailing Stop, Stop-Limit, Algorithmic Orders, OCO (One-Cancels-Other)
- **Execution Model:** **Market Maker / STP hybrid** — Saxo acts as principal for some trades and routes others to liquidity providers
- **Average Execution Speed:** **9–10ms** average FX market-order fills; 99.3–99.5% completed in under 50ms
- **Slippage Analysis:** ~55–60% of EUR/USD orders filled with zero slippage; ~20% experienced negative slippage
- **Market Depth:** Available on SaxoTraderPRO
- **Hedging:** Allowed
- **Scalping:** Allowed but not optimized (slippage more noticeable under 300-400ms latency)

### 4.1 SaxoTraderGO

- **Status:** Saxo's flagship multi-asset trading platform (launched May 2015)
- **Features:** Advanced charting, multi-asset trading (FX, stocks, ETFs, futures, options, bonds), risk management tools, integrated research, customizable watchlists
- **Mobile:** iOS and Android — strong functionality
- **WebTrader:** Full web-based platform
- **Assessment:** Industry-leading platform experience; ForexBrokers.com #1 Desktop Platform winner
- **Limitation:** Can't drag-to-edit orders on charts

### 4.2 SaxoTraderPRO

- **Status:** Professional-grade platform for high-volume traders
- **Features:** Multi-screen support, algorithmic orders, advanced risk tools, Level 2 pricing, customizable layouts
- **Assessment:** Premium experience for professionals; lacks quick-start layouts

### 4.3 SaxoInvestor

- **Status:** Simplified platform for long-term investors
- **Features:** Stocks, ETFs, bonds, managed portfolios (SaxoSelect)

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

Saxo Bank A/S was founded in 1992 in Copenhagen, Denmark, by Kim Fournais and Lars Seier Christensen. The company began as a small brokerage firm called Midas Fondsmæglerselskab, which was later renamed Saxo Bank when it obtained its banking license in 2001. This banking license is a critical differentiator — Saxo Bank is not just a broker but a fully licensed bank, subject to the stringent capital adequacy and regulatory requirements of banking regulation.

From its Copenhagen headquarters, Saxo Bank has grown into one of the world's premier multi-asset trading and investment firms, serving over 850,000 clients globally with access to more than 70,000 financial instruments across forex, stocks, bonds, ETFs, options, futures, and cryptocurrencies. The company manages assets exceeding DKK 700 billion (~$100B+) and maintains offices in London, Singapore, Hong Kong, Dubai, Tokyo, Sydney, and other major financial centers.

Saxo Bank's platform technology has been recognized with numerous industry awards. The company offers two proprietary platforms — SaxoTraderGO (web-based) and SaxoTraderPRO (desktop) — as well as TradingView integration and FIX API access for institutional clients. The platforms provide institutional-grade tools including advanced charting, algorithmic trading, and comprehensive market research.

The ownership structure includes Danish billionaire Kim Fournais (47%), Geely Financials (19.4%), and other institutional investors. This private ownership provides stability without quarterly earnings pressure.
